title: Jagged Memories
lines: Your fingers graze the jagged wooden walls. The surface is unnaturally cold and feels less like wood and more like petrified bone.
As you touch it, you feel a faint, pulsing warmth from within the walls, and for a moment, you see a flash of a vast, star-swept desert that you've never visited.
The wood seems to be drinking in the heat from your fingertips.

options.desc: Keep your hands close and keep moving
